package.name: bringup
package.description:
Top-level launch files for the IFSSIM autonomy pipeline.
Hosts three composed launch files plus the shared launch helpers they all use:
full_pipeline.launch.py — UE5 bridge + foxglove + bag recorder + management trio (mode_manager / mission_control / sim_supervisor) + autonomy lifecycle nodes (odometry through control). This is what docker/dv_pipeline_stack runs at container startup.
sim_pipeline.launch.py — management trio + autonomy lifecycle nodes. No UE5 bridge, no foxglove, no bag recorder; for developers running the autonomy locally against an externally-launched simulator or bag replay.
car_pipeline.launch.py — mode_manager + mission_control + autonomy wired onto the real-vehicle surface (autonomy_actions(profile="car")). The uDV (a micro-ROS endpoint) is the mission_control peer directly, over the stock-typed interface in topic_contract.py (/assi/state + /ami/mission in, /dv/status + /ctrl/cmd + /force_ebs out) — no car-side adapter nodes, no sim_supervisor, no bridge. See docs/CAR_ADAPTATION.md.
The shared lifecycle-launch helpers (`_auto_active`, `_autonomy_lifecycle`) and the IFSSIM /fsds/* topic remap constants live in bringup/launch_common.py so the three launch files compose the same node-graph fragments without duplicating boilerplate.
